title: Entschuldigung - Quick Summary
items: **Learn this one word first: Entschuldigung.** It covers excuse me, sorry and pardon me, all in one word, and it works in almost every situation.
**Say it like this: ent-SHOOL-dee-goong**, stress on the second part. Berliners often shorten it to just 'tschuldigung in casual speech.
**Use it to get past someone**, on U-Bahn/S-Bahn doors, in museum corridors, or through a market crowd. People make room almost automatically.
**Use it to open a question**, before asking a Späti owner, a waiter, or a stranger for directions, rather than starting straight in English.
**It beats English 'sorry'** because there is no half-second confusion before the other person switches languages to answer you.
**Save Tut mir leid for genuine regret** and Verzeihung for a more formal register. As a visitor, Entschuldigung covers nearly every moment on its own.
